mnemonics: ARMA models are the LEGO bricks for building forecasts. AR pieces (past forecasts) and MA pieces (past shocks) snap together to form the final prediction.
keyPoints: The forecast function for an ARMA model is a weighted average of past observations and past forecast errors.
As the forecast horizon (h) increases, the forecast converges to the unconditional mean of the series if it is stationary.
The updating process occurs when a new observation becomes available; the new forecast incorporates this new information and the previous forecast error.
commonMistakes: Assuming forecasts remain uncertain; forecast error variance increases for longer horizons.
Forgetting to check for stationarity; non-stationary series (like those with a trend) will not revert to a mean.
Mixing up the AR and MA components; AR uses past values of the series, MA uses past values of the error (shock).
